content:
Let me analyze this task: 1. This is stage web-001-home (Order 1) 2. I need to create webpage 1 as index.html with: - A title - Main content - An empty navigation area that can grow later 3. The validators mention: - file-exists: failure_class=missing_artifact - html-parse: failure_class=syntax 4. Required pages mentioned (though this is stage 1, so I might only need index.html): - index.html: title must include 'Entropy Page 1' - page-2.html through page-5.html with specific requirements 5. From page order 4, every page must link to index.html, page-2.html, page-3.html, and page-4.html 6. By stage 5, index.html and page-5.html must include data-entropy-shared-table="factory-links" Since this is stage 1 (web-001-home), I should focus on creating index.html with: - Title containing 'Entropy Page 1' - Main content - Empty navigation area Let me create the index.html file: ```html Entropy Page 1

Welcome to Entropy Page 1

This is the main content area.

``` Wait, I need to check if I should create all pages or just index.html. The stage goal says "Create webpage 1 as index.html" - so it seems like I should focus on index.html for now. But the validators mention all pages... Let me re-read. Since this is stage 1 and the goal is specifically to create webpage 1 as index.html, I'll focus on that single file with the required title containing 'Entropy Page 1', main content, and an empty navigation area. The other pages appear to be part of a larger validation system, but my immediate task is just index.html for this stage. { "files": [ { "path": "site/index.html", "content": "\n\n\n \n \n Entropy Page 1\n\n\n
\n
\n

Welcome to Entropy Page 1

\n

This is the main content area for the first page.

\n
\n\n" } ], "notes": "Created index.html with title containing 'Entropy Page 1', main content, and empty navigation area for future growth." }
